title: Please generate a Standard independent contractor agreement template that is compliant in California

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Please generate a Standard independent contractor agreement template that is compliant in California

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
This Independent Contractor Agreement (this "Agreement") is made and entered into as of ______________ (the "Effective Date") by and between ____________________________, a California company, with its principal office located at __________________________________________________ (the "Company"), and ____________________________________________________, a contractor residing at __________________________________________________ (the "Contractor"), individually referred to as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

1. Services.
The Contractor shall provide the Company with services (the "Services") as described in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein by this reference. The Contractor will determine the method, details, and means of performing the Services.

2. Term.
This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and continue until terminated by either Party pursuant to Section 6 of this Agreement.

3. Independent Contractor Relationship.
The relationship between the Parties is that of an independent contractor, and nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, employer-employee, or agency relationship. The Contractor is not authorized to represent the Company or bind the Company in any manner.

4. Compensation.
In consideration of the Services, the Company shall pay the Contractor fees as set forth in Exhibit A (the "Fees"). The Contractor shall be responsible for all federal, state, and local taxes, insurance premiums, or other expenses incurred in connection with the Services and the receipt of the Fees.

5. Confidentiality.
The Contractor agrees to keep confidential and not disclose to third parties or use, except in the performance of the Services, all proprietary or confidential information disclosed by Company to Contractor.

6. Termination.
Either Party may terminate this Agreement upon written notice to the other Party if the other Party breaches any material term or condition of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within fourteen (14) days after receipt of written notice thereof.

7. Work Product.
All ideas, works of authorship, inventions, discoveries, improvements, processes, developments, and other result of the Services of Contractor (collectively, the "Work Product") shall be deemed a "work made for hire" under applicable copyright law, and the Contractor shall retain no ownership or other rights therein. To the extent the foregoing is deemed by applicable law not to be a self-executing transfer, the Contractor hereby assigns to the Company all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product.

8. Indemnification.
The Contractor agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Company and its affiliates, officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any and all losses, liabilities, claims, causes of action, damages, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorney's fees and costs of investigation) arising out of or in connection with any breach of this Agreement by the Contractor, including but not limited to any claim by a third party arising out of or in connection with the Services or the Work Product.

9. Governing Law.
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflicts of law principles.

10. Entire Agreement.
This Agreement, including Exhibit A, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, representations, and warranties, both written and oral, with respect to such subject matter.

11. Amendment.
This Agreement may only be amended, modified, or supplemented by an agreement in writing signed by each Party hereto.

12. Notices.
All notices or other communications under this Agreement must be in writing and shall be deemed given when delivered personally, sent via email (receipt confirmed), or mailed by certified mail, return receipt requested, to the Parties at the addresses set forth above, or to such other address as each party may designate by notice hereunder.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties hereto have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

_______________________________          _______________________________
Company [Name]                                      Contractor [Name]
By:                                                        By:
Title:                                                      Title:

EXHIBIT A

Description of Services: [Detailed description of services to be provided by Contractor]
Fees: [Specify rates, retainer or other terms of Compensation]
Term: [Start date and end date, if applicable]

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Please generate a Standard employment agreement template that is complaint in California

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT

This Employment Agreement (the “Agreement”) is entered into as of __________ (the “Effective Date”), by and between ____________________________, a California company, with its principal office located at __________________________________________________ (the “Company”), and ____________________________________________________, an individual residing at __________________________________________________ (the “Employee”), collectively referred to as the “Parties.”

1. Position
The Company agrees to employ Employee in the position of ______________. Employee shall perform such duties and responsibilities as are customarily associated with such position, and as the Company may assign from time to time. Employee shall report to _____________ or such other person as the Company may designate.

2. At-Will Employment
The employment of Employee shall be “at-will.” Either the Company or Employee may terminate the employment relationship at any time and for any reason, with or without cause and with or without prior notice. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to alter the at-will nature of the employment relationship.

3. Work Schedule
Employee’s normal work schedule shall be __________ hours per week, from _____ to _____, unless otherwise agreed by the Company and Employee.

4. Compensation
Employee shall receive a salary of $_________ per year, payable in accordance with the Company’s regular payroll practices. Employee shall also be eligible to participate in any bonus, incentive, or other compensation plans offered by the Company from time to time, subject to the terms of such plans and the discretion of the Company.

5. Benefits
During the term of Employee’s employment, Employee shall be eligible to participate in the Company’s benefit plans and programs, subject to the terms and conditions of such plans and the discretion of the Company.

6. Vacation and Sick Leave
Employee shall be entitled to accrue paid vacation and sick leave in accordance with the Company’s policies and applicable California law.

7. Confidentiality and Proprietary Rights
Employee agrees to keep confidential and not disclose to third parties, except as necessary in the normal course of Employee’s employment, any proprietary or confidential information of the Company. Employee further agrees that all ideas, works of authorship, inventions, discoveries, improvements, processes, developments, and other results of Employee’s work for the Company (collectively, the “Work Product”) shall be the exclusive property of the Company, and Employee hereby assigns to the Company all right, title, and interest in and to the Work Product.

8. Termination
The Parties acknowledge and agree that the employment relationship is at-will and may be terminated at any time by either Party for any reason, with or without cause and with or without prior notice.

9. Governing Law
This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflicts of law principles.

10. Entire Agreement
This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings, agreements, representations, and warranties, both written and oral, with respect to such subject matter.

11. Amendment
This Agreement may only be amended or modified by a writing signed by both Parties.

12. Notices
All notices, requests, consents, claims, demands, waivers, and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be deemed to have been given: (a) when delivered by hand (with written confirmation of receipt); (b) when received by the addressee if sent by a nationally recognized overnight courier (with written confirmation of receipt); or (c) on the date sent by email or facsimile (with confirmation of transmission) if sent during normal business hours on a business day (or on the next business day if sent after normal business hours, or on a day that is not a business day).

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

_______________________________          _______________________________
Company [Name]                                      Employee [Name]
By:                                                                              By:
Title:                                                                           Title:

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: please generate an annual minutes template with a section about the acquisition of new fixed assets for an LLC

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
ANNUAL MINUTES OF THE MEETING OF THE MEMBERS AND MANAGERS
OF [LLC NAME]

The annual meeting of the Members and Managers of [LLC Name], a [State] limited liability company (the "Company"), was held on [Date] at [Time], at the principal office of the Company located at [Address], or at such other place as the Managers may designate from time to time.

1. Call to Order

[Name of Presiding Manager] called the meeting to order and welcomed everyone present. The following Members and Managers were present at the meeting:

[List of Managers and Members present]

2. Confirmation of Notice and Quorum

The notice of the meeting was confirmed, and it was determined that a quorum of the Members and Managers was present.

3. Approval of the Minutes of the Previous Meeting

The minutes of the last annual meeting held on [Date of previous annual meeting] were reviewed. There being no corrections or additions, a motion was made, seconded, and carried to approve the minutes as presented.

4. Financial Report

[Name of Manager or Member responsible for the financial report] presented the financial report for the year ended [Date]. The report included a balance sheet, income statement, and cash flow statement. After discussion, the financial report was approved as presented. A copy of the financial report shall be attached to these minutes.

5. Acquisition of New Fixed Assets

[Name of Manager or Member presenting the proposal] presented a proposal for the acquisition of new fixed assets for the Company. The proposed fixed assets were as follows:

[List the fixed assets and any relevant details, such as cost, plans for financing or leasing, expected useful life, etc.]

Discussion ensued, and relevant matters were considered, including the costs, financing options, potential benefits, and risks associated with the acquisition of the proposed fixed assets.

Upon proper motion and second, it was unanimously:

RESOLVED, that the Company is authorized to acquire the new fixed assets described in the proposal, and the Managers are authorized and directed to take such actions and execute such documents as may be necessary or appropriate to effectuate the acquisition, financing, and any other related matters.

6. Other Business

[Name of Presiding Manager] asked if there was any other business to be brought before the meeting. There being none,

7. Adjournment

A motion was made, seconded, and carried to adjourn the meeting. The meeting was adjourned at [Time].

Dated: ________________________

Approved By:

_______________________________
[Manager/Member Name]

Attached:
- Financial Report
- Acquisition Proposal (if applicable)
